description: Facts about target tractability, druggability assessment, and therapeutic target selection

facts.context: For small molecule druggability, a protein target requires:
facts.truth: a defined binding pocket or active site for ligand binding
distractors: a transmembrane domain for membrane penetration
post-translational modifications for recognition
secretion into extracellular space for accessibility

facts.context: Antibody therapeutics are most suitable when the target is:
facts.truth: cell surface or secreted with extracellular accessibility
distractors: intracellular with nuclear localization
cytoplasmic with rapid turnover
mitochondrial with membrane embedding

facts.context: Kinases are highly druggable because they possess:
facts.truth: conserved ATP-binding pockets amenable to competitive inhibition
distractors: unique substrate binding sites requiring covalent modification
no enzymatic activity to inhibit
constitutive membrane localization for antibody access

facts.context: Transcription factors are challenging to drug because they:
facts.truth: lack well-defined binding pockets for small molecule interaction
distractors: are always nuclear and inaccessible
have very short half-lives preventing drug binding
require DNA binding for all their functions

facts.truth: degradation of previously undruggable targets via ubiquitin-proteasome
distractors: activation of previously inactive enzyme targets
membrane insertion of cytoplasmic proteins
gene editing of pathogenic variants

facts.context: GPCRs (G-protein coupled receptors) are excellent drug targets because:
facts.truth: they have orthosteric and allosteric binding sites accessible from extracellular space
distractors: they lack any endogenous ligands to compete with
they are exclusively intracellular proteins
they have no conformational changes upon ligand binding

facts.context: Ion channels are druggable by small molecules that:
facts.truth: bind to the channel pore or regulatory domains modulating conductance
distractors: replace the channel protein entirely
bind only to associated scaffolding proteins
require membrane disruption for access

facts.context: For loss-of-function mutations, the preferred therapeutic approach is:
facts.truth: protein replacement, gene therapy, or mRNA to restore function
distractors: small molecule inhibitors to block residual activity
antibodies to neutralize the defective protein
antisense oligonucleotides to further reduce expression

facts.context: For gain-of-function mutations, the preferred therapeutic approach is:
facts.truth: inhibition via small molecules, antibodies, or knockdown strategies
distractors: gene therapy to add more copies of the mutant gene
mRNA therapy to increase expression
protein replacement to compete with mutant

facts.context: Proteases are highly druggable because they:
facts.truth: have well-defined active site catalytic triads amenable to inhibition
distractors: are always secreted into extracellular space
have no enzymatic activity to target
require only allosteric modulation

facts.context: Secreted proteins can be targeted by:
facts.truth: neutralizing antibodies that bind and sequester the protein
distractors: small molecules that penetrate cell membranes
intracellular gene editing approaches
nuclear receptor modulators

facts.context: Protein-protein interactions are difficult to drug because:
facts.truth: interfaces are typically flat and lack deep binding pockets
distractors: the proteins are too small for any ligand binding
interactions occur only in the nucleus
binding affinity is always too weak to disrupt

facts.context: Allosteric modulators offer advantages over orthosteric inhibitors by:
facts.truth: providing greater selectivity and preserving physiological regulation
distractors: always having higher affinity than orthosteric ligands
being effective only for intracellular targets
avoiding all off-target effects entirely

facts.context: Validated drug targets are those with:
facts.truth: approved drugs demonstrating clinical efficacy against the target
distractors: only computational predictions of druggability
expression changes in disease tissue
genetic association studies only

facts.context: Enzyme replacement therapy is suitable for diseases with:
facts.truth: deficiency of a secreted or lysosomal enzyme that can be exogenously supplied
distractors: overactive cytoplasmic enzymes requiring inhibition
membrane-bound enzymes with no secreted form
nuclear enzymes that cannot cross cell membranes

facts.truth: prolonged target engagement and resistance to competition are needed
distractors: rapid reversibility is required for safety
the target has no nucleophilic residues
off-target effects must be minimized
